Document Metadata.Title: A System of Effective Transmission Data for Rating Telephone Circuits
Document Metadata.Engineering Domain: Telecommunications — Telephone transmission systems and circuit performance evaluation

Technical Dictionary Terms.Definition: A quantitative measure representing the transmission performance of a telephone circuit expressed in decibels relative to a reference circuit.
Technical Dictionary Terms.Context: Used to evaluate overall transmission performance by combining distortion, noise, and sidetone effects.

Technical Dictionary Terms.Definition: A reference circuit arrangement used as a standard for comparing transmission performance of telephone circuits.
Technical Dictionary Terms.Context: Allows circuit element changes to be expressed as changes in effective equivalent of the reference system.

Technical Dictionary Terms.Definition: Loss in transmission level expressed in decibels relative to a reference circuit.
Technical Dictionary Terms.Context: Used as the basis for rating circuit performance and evaluating circuit elements.

Technical Dictionary Terms.Definition: Loss associated with the transmitting loop of a telephone connection.
Technical Dictionary Terms.Context: Component contributing to effective circuit loss.

Technical Dictionary Terms.Definition: Loss associated with the receiving loop in a telephone circuit.
Technical Dictionary Terms.Context: Used when calculating effective losses of circuits.

Technical Dictionary Terms.Definition: Sound heard in the transmitter originating from the receiver of the same telephone circuit.
Technical Dictionary Terms.Context: Considered a transmission characteristic affecting circuit performance.

Technical Dictionary Terms.Definition: Noise introduced by circuit components or external interference affecting transmission quality.
Technical Dictionary Terms.Context: Considered in determining effective transmission loss.

Technical Dictionary Terms.Definition: Environmental noise present at the receiving location affecting telephone transmission perception.
Technical Dictionary Terms.Context: Added to effective losses when evaluating receiving system performance.

Technical Dictionary Terms.Definition: Loss introduced at junction points between circuit sections.
Transmission loss associated with trunk lines connecting exchanges.
Technical Dictionary Terms.Context: Considered in rating telephone circuit elements.

Engineering Rules.Rule: The performance of a complete telephone circuit can be described with sufficient accuracy using volume loss, sidetone, distortion, and noise parameters.
Engineering Rules.Explanation: These parameters represent the major transmission characteristics influencing circuit performance.

Engineering Rules.Rule: Transmission performance should be expressed relative to a reference system to enable comparison between circuits.
Engineering Rules.Explanation: Using a reference system allows circuit element changes to be represented as changes in effective equivalent loss.

Engineering Rules.Rule: The effective transmission loss of a circuit is determined by combining the equivalent losses of individual circuit elements.
Engineering Rules.Explanation: Individual components such as loops, trunks, and junctions contribute to overall effective circuit loss.

Engineering Rules.Rule: Loss ratings of circuit elements are expressed relative to the loss of a reference loop.
Engineering Rules.Explanation: Each circuit element is evaluated relative to the performance of the reference system.

Engineering Rules.Rule: Changes in sidetone, distortion, or noise can be expressed as equivalent changes in volume loss.
Engineering Rules.Explanation: This allows different transmission impairments to be represented within a single rating metric.

Engineering Parameters.Meaning: Transmission loss relative to reference circuit
Engineering Parameters.Effect: Higher loss reduces signal strength and clarity

Engineering Parameters.Meaning: Degree to which speaker hears their own voice
Engineering Parameters.Effect: Excessive sidetone reduces perceived transmission quality

Engineering Parameters.Meaning: Deviation of signal from original waveform

Engineering Parameters.Effect: Influences transmission loss and sidetone characteristics
